raw_consumer_statement:
I

got an alert from Citi Cards about potential fraudulent transactions and when I checked, I called right away and let them know I didn't recognize the last 3 transactions. They proceeded to put a dispute and sent me a new credit card. When I received my statement, the fraudulent transactions were still there and I was told that it would take 1 to 2 billing cycles to clear them, which I think is unacceptable, especially when they amount to {$1300.00} and if not paid, they will charge steep interest rates. While on hold, I noticed that there was 2 other transactions that I had not authorized on my new credit card. I was told that for certain vendors, Citi automatically updates the number card number, and that I had it set up this way on my account. When asked how I would disactive this function, they told me I could not do it myself, and only Citi had that capability. In another words, they made it easy for the thieves to continue shopping on XXXX with my stolen identity.

Also, I was told that I could not dispute the new fraudulent charge because the transaction was pending, and it wasn't until I called again that they agreed to dispute it.

They're sending me another new credit card, but in the meantime, I have to pay {$1600.00} in fraudulent charges so that I would not be charged interest.

There's got to be a better way to protect the consumer. I'm lucky I could cover these charges, but what about the families that lives paycheck to paycheck?

Citi should reverse charges immediately, while transaction is being disputed. They have neough money to extend this courtesy to all their customers.
